next_action: Ask exactly one concise A/B/C decision card selecting how Stage 1 and Stage 2 are bounded against indefinite expansion: no hard timebox and continue until quality is satisfactory; one fixed calendar deadline for each whole stage; or milestone budgets with predeclared review caps for core mechanics, prototype environment, developer validation, external validation, and Stage 2 end-to-end production proof, where missed caps trigger written scope reduction, diagnosis, resequencing, or stop review rather than automatic overtime. Preserve the two-stage evidence gates, predeclared pass signals, pivot-or-stop rule, solo-led AI-assisted production, one-level internal slice, and exact calendar durations as planning variables rather than fixed requirements. Include part-time schedule uncertainty, hidden technical risk, architecture overbuilding, quality pressure, sunk-cost behavior, incomplete evidence, dependencies, what counts as a justified extension, and how extensions are approved without silently moving the gate. Put the recommendation after all A/B/C options at the end of the card. Begin a new formal persistence batch D-231 through D-240 unless the user changes the cadence or requests a handoff.
